user_query:
I live in Kentucky (US/Eastern) and I have multiple sclerosis. I need a small, local scheduling setup that creates medication reminder files only during the day and also produces a weekly symptom summary I can share with my neurologist. Please use the files in this workspace and produce the outputs exactly where specified.

Inputs provided:
- config/settings.yaml (has blanks you must fill)
- input/med_schedule.csv (my med times)
- input/symptom_log.csv (two weeks of symptom entries)

What I need:
1) Modify config/settings.yaml so that:
   - timezone is set to "US/Eastern".
   - quiet_hours.start is "07:00" and quiet_hours.end is "21:00" (I don’t want reminders at night).
   Keep all other paths and values intact. Your code must read and honor these settings.

2) Create a script at tools/med_scheduler.py that does all of the following when run from the repo root:
   a) Schedule preview and reminder files (simulation mode driven by config.simulation):
      - Read input/med_schedule.csv and generate occurrences for the date range [simulation.start_date, simulation.start_date + simulation.days - 1].
      - Only include rows whose weekday appears in the CSV "days" field and times that fall within quiet hours (inclusive) from settings.yaml.
      - Write a CSV at output/schedule_preview.csv with columns: date,time_local,medication,dose,instructions,source_row. Sort by date, then time_local. time_local should be the time string from the med schedule interpreted in the configured timezone.
      - For each scheduled occurrence in the simulation range, also write a TXT file under output/reminders/YYYY-MM-DD/ named HHMM_[medication].txt. The file should contain a single line: "Take {medication} {dose} at {time_local}". Create directories as needed.

   b) Weekly symptom summary with ranking:
      - Read input/symptom_log.csv and compute a severity_score for each date as: 0.5*fatique + 0.3*pain + 0.2*mobility (round to 2 decimals). Assume higher is worse.
      - Group by ISO week (Monday start). Within each week, rank days by severity_score (1 = highest). Tie-break by earlier date first.
      - Write output/weekly_summary.csv with columns: week_start,date,fatigue,pain,mobility,severity_score,rank_in_week, including ONLY the top 3 days per week (rank_in_week <= 3). Sort by week_start then rank_in_week.

3) Run your script so that the following artifacts are produced now in this workspace:
   - output/schedule_preview.csv for the configured simulation window.
   - The corresponding reminder TXT files in output/reminders/YYYY-MM-DD/ for each simulated occurrence.
   - output/weekly_summary.csv generated from all rows of input/symptom_log.csv.

Checks I will use:
- The schedule preview must show only times between 07:00 and 21:00 inclusive and reflect the provided med days/times. Sorting should be by date then time.
- The weekly summary must use the exact formula above, group by ISO week (Monday start), and list exactly the top 3 days per week with correct ranks and sorting.

All work should be self-contained and runnable locally from the command line. Please make sure modifying config/settings.yaml is essential (your script must read it), and that you actually run the script to create the outputs at the specified paths. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
